name: Butterfly Shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove shells from shrimp. For deveining, insert a knife into the back of each shrimp and cut crosswise without severing it completely; leave some connection at head and tail ends. Open shrimp halves to form butterfly shape and remove vein. Repeat with remaining shrimp. Optionally, flatten using flat edge of Chinese cleaver.
2. Dust butterfly shrimp on both sides with corn starch placed on wax paper. Place coated shrimp on another piece of wax paper to dry slightly.
3. Mix 1 Egg, 2/3 cup Water, 2/3 cup All purpose flour, and Salt in separate bowl for batter. Set aside.
4. Heat oil in wok until hot enough to brown bread lightly when placed in the oil without smoking. If bread browns too quickly, oil is too hot; if it doesn't brown at all, heat the oil further. Allow sufficient time for oil to cool before reuse.
5. Coat butterfly shrimp with batter using chopsticks or fork. Carefully slide each shrimp into the oil and cook until golden brown for about 1-1/2 minutes. Flip shrimp and continue frying on other side for another 1-1/2 minutes. Remove cooked shrimp to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
6. Serve cooked butterfly shrimp on serving platter, garnished with parsley or other raw vegetables.
NOTE: Corn starch coating helps the batter adhere to shrimp during cooking. Without this layer, the batter may fall off easily after frying.
